scope_note: QA Witt Tower tau-Monotone Discrimination Ladder Cert [503]. CLAIM: The six Witt Tower empirical certs [491][495][490][494][492][493] form a monotone concordant discrimination ladder: domain autocorrelation timescale tau (ordinal rank 1..6: EEG < FX < rivers < precip < temp < SST) and observed n_sig_ratio (return-rank n_signal/n_expected) are Kendall-concordant for all C(6,2)=15 pairs. (WTM_1) 6 domains declared with tau_rank 1..6 distinct, n_sig_milli=n_sig_ratio*1000 positive integer milliunits, cert_ids referencing [491][495][490][494][492][493]; (WTM_2) all 15 tau_rank/n_sig_milli pairs concordant — if tau_rank_i < tau_rank_j then n_sig_milli_i < n_sig_milli_j (Kendall tau = 1, pure integer comparison); (WTM_3) anti/null/structural split: EEG n_sig_milli < 1000 (anti-persistent), FX n_sig_milli in [1000,2000) (null zone), rivers/precip/temp/SST n_sig_milli >= 2000 (structural persistence); (WTM_4) ladder span ratio > 6.0 (max_n_sig_milli * 1000 > min_n_sig_milli * 6000, integer check). Physical mechanism: tau ordering reflects autocorrelation timescales (Hasselmann 1976 ocean thermal inertia tau~months >> atmosphere tau~weeks >> rivers tau~days >> EEG tau<1s); return-rank operator amplifies persistence structure. NOT CLAIMED: exact tau values (ordinal claim only); causation direction; claim that QA causes the ladder (QA observes the ladder via the return-rank operator). Theorem NT: n_sig_ratio is count/count (integer ratio from empirical certs); continuous autocorrelation coefficient is observer projection, not QA state. All arithmetic pure integer. Companion empirical certs: [491] EEG (0.72x), [495] FX (1.009x), [490] rivers (2.69x), [494] precip (3.05x), [492] temp (3.40x), [493] SST (4.43x). Primary: Wildberger N.J. (2005) ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8; Hasselmann K. (1976) doi:10.1111/j.2153-3490.1976.tb00696.x. Derived 2026-06-23.
